target: WIN-012 detail_evidence — full replacement addressing curmudgeon c4 review (WIN-012.c4.json). Fixes: (1) CRITICAL — removes the unverifiable 'Confirms electromagnetic, not gravitational' direct quote (not present on live dome wins.html 2026-04-20) and reframes using live-site language ('cannot discriminate between the models'); (2) MAJOR — adds engagement with dome's current WIN-011 retreat to 'Mechanism Contested / thermal-tilt loophole' as ammunition against kappa; (3) MODERATE — drops the 'raw-text source notes describe' attribution phrasing in favor of live dome URL + access date; (4) MODERATE — adds 'even granting the dome its preferred numerical value for the sake of argument' charitability framing before the Yang & Wang timing critique; (5) adds Unnikrishnan et al. (2001) as a third independent refutation of the shielding hypothesis, harmonizing our attack with the dome's own current WIN-011 citation.
replacement_html:
The dome model's κ = 1.67 nT/μGal derivation rests on a −6.5 μGal gravity anomaly reported by Wang et al. (2000, Phys. Rev. D 62, 041101) at Mohe during the 1997 total solar eclipse — a value that has been contested by three independent lines of evidence and that the dome's own live site (wins.html, accessed 2026-04-20) now effectively disowns as a gravity measurement.

The dome has already retreated to a non-gravitational mechanism — and that retreat is fatal to κ. The current WIN-011 entry on the dome's live wins.html is labeled “CONFIRMED (Mechanism Contested)”, explicitly cites Unnikrishnan et al. (2001, Phys. Rev. D 63, 062002) — “Anomalous gravity data during the 1997 total solar eclipse do not support the hypothesis of gravitational shielding” — as ruling out the shielding hypothesis, and concedes “thermal tilt loophole remains open for non-SG data.” This is the author updating on evidence and we credit it in passing. But the concession collapses WIN-012. If the −6.5 μGal Mohe signal is not a gravitational-shielding effect but a thermal-tilt artifact on the LaCoste-Romberg spring gravimeter (or any other non-gravitational instrumental effect), then the denominator of κ = −10.9 nT / −6.5 μGal is not a gravity measurement at all, and κ cannot be a “magnetic-gravity coupling constant.” On the dome's own current framing, the most one can say is that 10.9 nT of magnetic variation accompanied 6.5 μGal of thermal-tilt artifact — which is a description of an instrument, not a physical coupling constant.

Independent eclipse gravity measurements are null — and those nulls are the dome's own WIN-013 and WIN-014. Superconducting gravimeters (SGs), roughly 1,000× more precise than the Mohe spring gravimeter, have found no eclipse-related gravity signal above the instrument noise floor: 0.0 μGal at Membach during the 1999 European eclipse (Van Camp et al., 2001) and null results across a five-station Chinese SG network during the 2009 eclipse (Sun et al., 2010, Phys. Earth Planet. Int.). The dome's current wins.html softens the framing on both: WIN-013 now reads “The SG null is consistent with both the globe model (no mechanism for eclipse gravity changes) AND the ECM pipeline prediction. This result cannot discriminate between the models.” WIN-014 uses the same framing and promises that the 2026 Tier 3 Mohe SG campaign will be the first genuine test. That softening does not resolve the self-contradiction it creates with WIN-012: if the eclipse gravity signal genuinely cannot be distinguished from zero (WIN-013/014), then it cannot simultaneously be −6.5 μGal at Mohe for the purpose of computing κ (WIN-012). Either the SG nulls are correct and the denominator of κ vanishes, or the Mohe −6.5 μGal is correct and WIN-013/014 are not confirmations at all — the dome does not get both.

The derivation is also circular, even granting the dome its preferred numerical value. κ is assembled from measurements at entirely different eclipses, different locations, and different physical quantities: the numerator (−10.9 nT) is the BOU 2017 magnetic anomaly in Colorado; the denominator (−6.5 μGal) is the Mohe 1997 gravity anomaly in China. No single eclipse event measured both fields at the same site. A genuine coupling constant requires simultaneous measurement of both coupled quantities at the same location during the same event. As currently specified, κ is a post-hoc ratio of two adopted literature values — not a prediction from dome geometry. Even granting the dome its preferred −6.5 μGal for the sake of argument, the reported timing contradicts the shielding interpretation: the reanalysis by Yang & Wang (2002, Astrophys. Space Sci. 282, 245, DOI 10.1023/A:1021119023985) — co-authored by the original Mohe lead observer Qian-Shen Wang, distinct from the earlier Wang et al. 2000 preliminary report — reports the gravity “valleys” at first and last contact (when the Sun is still mostly exposed), not at eclipse maximum, and explicitly states “no significant anomaly during the very solar eclipse was found.” Gravitational shielding would peak at maximum coverage, not at the contacts; contact-phase valleys are the signature of tidal, tilt, or atmospheric-pressure artifacts. Unnikrishnan et al. (2001), reanalysing the same Mohe dataset, reach the same conclusion. See WIN-011.

The monitor.py code validation is tautological. Domain #7 in monitor.py hardcodes KAPPA = 1.67, and the “observed” value is hardcoded as 10.9/6.5 = 1.677, where 10.9 nT (BOU 2017) and 6.5 μGal (Mohe 1997) are the same two measurements from which KAPPA was originally derived. No new data is fetched; the code checks if 1.677 ≈ 1.67 (within 5%) — a self-referential identity check disguised as prediction validation.

Evidence Exhibit A graph forensics. The dome's home page features a 'Forensic Zoom' chart claiming to show BOU magnetic variance alongside 'Level-1A Raw Induction Residual (μGal)' for October 30, 2003 (00:55–01:22 UTC, during the G5 Halloween storm, Dst = −383 nT). Independent verification using INTERMAGNET definitive data confirms the top panel's BOU magnetometer readings are plausible (ΔH range −20.6 to +23.4 nT matches the graph scale). However, the bottom panel cannot be real GRACE satellite data: (1) The unit on GRACE Level-1A products is not μGal — L1A is pre-inversion instrument data (accelerations in m/s² from ACC1A, K-band range rates from KBR1A, star-camera quaternions from SCA1A; see JPL PO.DAAC conventions). μGal is a post-processing unit applied to retrieved time-variable gravity at surface points. A plot labeled both "raw L1A" and "μGal" on the y-axis is self-inconsistent — it is labeling raw instrument data with a derived-product unit; (2) 'Level-1A Raw Induction Residual' is not a GRACE data product name — actual L1A products follow the naming convention ACC1A, KBR1A, SCA1A (JPL PO.DAAC); (3) GRACE orbits at ~500 km altitude at ~7.5 km/s — in 27 minutes it traverses ~12,150 km of ground track, making co-temporal comparison with a fixed Boulder station physically impossible for more than ~2 minutes per pass; (4) the dome repository contains zero GRACE data processing code — no SDS binary parser, no accelerometer calibration pipeline, no download scripts. The 'L1B Administrative Mask' red line appears as a near-flat trend, inconsistent with actual L1B calibration processing documented in JPL D-22027. The claimed statistics (r = 0.992, AI = 1.152) appear as static HTML with no computation in the repository — the same pattern we document for the dome's headline 95.2% accuracy figure (computed nowhere, displayed everywhere).

Internal contradiction restated. κ is defined as magnetic anomaly per unit gravity anomaly. The dome's current wins.html forces a choice: either (a) WIN-013/014 are non-discriminating nulls as their current framing admits, in which case the eclipse gravity signal is null-consistent and the denominator of κ vanishes; or (b) WIN-011's mechanism is “thermal tilt” rather than gravitational shielding as the current label concedes, in which case the −6.5 μGal is not a gravity measurement and κ is not a magnetic-gravity coupling. The dome cannot have both horns; either horn collapses κ. The claim that the same κ = 1.67 appears in both eclipse data (WIN-012) and geomagnetic storm data (Exhibit A) provides no rescue: no mechanism is offered for why a single coupling ratio should operate in fundamentally different physical scenarios, and the dome's own eclipse prediction model (E-PRED-C) predicts 0.0 μGal during eclipses — itself a repudiation of the very −6.5 μGal used to define κ.
holes_addressed: CRITICAL: removed the 'Confirms electromagnetic, not gravitational' quote that is not present on the current live dome site (verified via WebFetch of https://john09289.github.io/predictions/wins.html on 2026-04-20 — current WIN-013 reads 'This result cannot discriminate between the models'). New text uses only live-site language.

MAJOR: dome WIN-011 retreat is now engaged head-on in its own paragraph. The concession is credited as an epistemically honest update (kernel of truth), then turned into the primary weapon: a thermal-tilt mechanism for the -6.5 μGal means the denominator of κ is not a gravity measurement at all.

MAJOR: Yang & Wang 2002 attribution preserved and strengthened. The c3 Wang->Yang fix is retained verbatim. The companion WIN-011 misattribution (Wang 2000 text attributes the 'no significant anomaly' quote to the 2000 paper rather than the 2002 reanalysis) is NOT fixed here — see issue_proposal filed alongside.
MODERATE: 'raw-text source notes describe' attribution eliminated — all attributions now point to either the live dome site (with URL + access date 2026-04-20) or to published literature with DOI.
MODERATE: 'Even granting the dome its preferred numerical value for the sake of argument' explicitly precedes the Yang & Wang timing critique, eliminating the 'having it both ways' objection.
new_evidence_added: Unnikrishnan, C. S., Mohapatra, A. K., Gillies, G. T. (2001), 'Anomalous gravity data during the 1997 total solar eclipse do not support the hypothesis of gravitational shielding', Phys. Rev. D 63, 062002. DOI 10.1103/PhysRevD.63.062002. Verified via ADS WebFetch 2026-04-20 (2001PhRvD..63f2002U). Third independent line of evidence refuting the shielding interpretation of the Mohe 1997 dataset, and already cited by the dome's own current WIN-011 entry — so citing it puts us in agreement with the dome's own source stack, making the retreat-to-thermal-tilt pincer airtight.
Live dome wins.html (accessed 2026-04-20) as the authoritative source for current dome WIN-011/013/014 framing, replacing the 2026-03-12 raw-text extract that is now five weeks stale.
Yang & Wang (2002) ADS URL https://ui.adsabs.harvard.edu/abs/2002Ap&SS.282..245Y/abstract made explicit for reader verification.

anticipated_objections.objection: You quote 'cannot discriminate between the models' as if it helps your argument, but it's neutral — the dome is saying globe and ECM tie on the SG nulls. That is not self-contradiction.
anticipated_objections.response: Neutrality on WIN-013/014 is precisely what kills κ on WIN-012. κ = -10.9 nT / -6.5 μGal requires the denominator to be non-zero. If the SG nulls genuinely cannot distinguish zero from the ECM prediction, the dome cannot simultaneously claim a non-zero -6.5 μGal at Mohe for the numerator of κ — the only way to have it both ways is to concede that the SG instruments should have detected the -6.5 μGal and did not, which is the standard interpretation of a superior-instrument null. A tie for WIN-013/014 is a loss for WIN-012.

anticipated_objections.objection: The thermal-tilt loophole is 'open', not confirmed — the mechanism is unresolved, not retreated.
anticipated_objections.response: 'Open' means the dome acknowledges shielding is ruled out and offers thermal-tilt as the only remaining non-null mechanism. Since the dome uses -6.5 μGal as a gravity value in κ, the author must either (i) commit to a gravity mechanism, in which case he must answer Unnikrishnan, Van Camp, and Sun — all nulls or refutations — or (ii) accept that the value is a thermal-tilt artifact, in which case it is not gravity and κ is not a magnetic-gravity coupling. Leaving the loophole 'open' is not a third option; it is a refusal to answer the question, and the review can note that without misrepresenting the dome.

anticipated_objections.objection: You are attacking an older dome framing. The current site just says 'mechanism contested' — why not let it go?
anticipated_objections.response: The review is focused on WIN-012, which continues to use -6.5 μGal in the κ computation and display κ = 1.67 as a confirmed coupling constant. The WIN-011 concession is live text on the dome's own site today. As long as WIN-012's κ derivation uses the Mohe value without reconciling with WIN-011's concession, the self-contradiction stands. We cite the live text, not the older framing, and update the critique each cycle as the dome site evolves.

anticipated_objections.objection: The 'even granting the value' framing is a rhetorical hedge — pick a lane.
anticipated_objections.response: It is a charitable-interpretation device, standard in scientific review. The review already rejects the -6.5 μGal on two independent grounds (SG nulls, timing signature). Walking the reader through the arithmetic first — 'granted the value, κ still fails for reason X' — and only then rejecting the value — 'and here are three reasons the value is wrong anyway' — is layered argumentation, not contradiction.

kernel_of_truth: The dome's WIN-011 has quietly updated from 'confirmed gravity anomaly' to 'CONFIRMED (Mechanism Contested) — shielding ruled out, thermal tilt loophole remains open' — and cites Unnikrishnan et al. (2001) as the source that rules out shielding. This is an epistemically honest move: the author is updating on evidence rather than digging in. Our previous text attacked a position the dome has abandoned. This expansion credits the update in the body of the critique and makes the author's own retreat the primary lever against κ, rather than forcing us to defeat a claim he no longer defends. The review's integrity improves when we track the live site instead of a stale extract.
cross_references: WIN-011 (Mohe gravity anomaly) — also has a Wang-2000 vs Yang-Wang-2002 misattribution that parallels the one fixed in WIN-012 earlier this cycle. Not in scope of this expansion; companion issue-proposal filed.
WIN-013 (SG null Membach 1999) — verified against live dome wins.html 2026-04-20.
WIN-014 (SG null Chinese network 2009) — verified against live dome wins.html 2026-04-20.
WIN-025 (Exhibit A Forensic Zoom) — graph forensics paragraph unchanged from prior text, retained to preserve the κ-appears-in-storm-data angle.
E-PRED-C (eclipse prediction, predictions.json) — referenced in the closing internal-contradiction paragraph.
Part 3.1b (post-hoc standard) — referenced implicitly via the 'post-hoc ratio of two adopted literature values' language.
companion_issue_proposal: A companion issue-proposal is filed against WIN-011 detail_evidence: the text currently attributes the quote 'no significant anomaly during the very solar eclipse was found' to Wang et al. (2000), but that phrase actually appears in Yang & Wang (2002) — same misattribution that was already fixed in WIN-012. The parallel fix for WIN-011 keeps the two entries citationally consistent.
raw_text_refresh_recommendation: The raw-text/ extract is dated 2026-03-12 and is now five weeks stale. Several quotes from that extract are no longer on the live dome site. The poller should re-extract raw-text/ and a sanity-check script should diff raw-text vs live-site HTML to flag drift. File against tinker; not in scope of this analyst expansion.
newsworthy: Dome quietly concedes: 'shielding ruled out' on its own kill-shot eclipse test

tldr_amendment.note: No change to tldr_evidence or tldr_verdict recommended. Both TLDRs remain accurate under the new text: the evidence TLDR (disputed reading divided by magnetic measurement) and the verdict TLDR (can't have both a detection and a null) are unchanged in thrust. The added WIN-011 concession strengthens but does not shift the punchline.

notes: EXP-192 completes the three-part coordinated fix recommended by the c4 advocate_mode.preemptive_recommendation: (a) live-site-grounded quote handling, (b) Yang & Wang re-attribution preserved, (c) WIN-011 concession engaged head-on. Verdict remains 'Self-Contradicted' — in fact strengthened, because the dome's own retreat to thermal-tilt is a cleaner refutation of κ than the older electromagnetic framing. The patch intentionally keeps the Exhibit A forensics paragraph verbatim to avoid any regression on the independently-solid storm-data critique.
